description: A straightforward Pinot Noir from Mendocino's inland Potter Valley, the wine is dominated by a layer of spearmint that overcomes the more nuanced flavors of cherry and raspberry.
title: Paul Dolan 2010 Pinot Noir (Potter Valley)

description: A solid and structured wine from this premier cru vineyard which is a Rodet monopoly. Dense and full in the mouth, the wine has delicious juicy fruits as well as powerful tannins and layers of acidity over the red fruit. Keep for 6–8 years.
title: Antonin Rodet 2010 Clos de Thorey Premier Cru  (Nuits-St.-Georges)

description: A very rich wine, spicy and complex. Dark fruits, rich tannins and layers of berries, exuding power and density. There is a sense of fine tannin as well, suspended in the texture of the wine. Firm and solid and with a great aging potential. Drink from 2019.
title: Château de Chambert 2010 Grand Vin Malbec (Cahors)

description: This is a very complete wine, with all the fruit and tannin elements in place. There's a fine and rich character here as well as a more austere, mineral side. The combination results in supporting tannins, dark fruits and good aging potential.
title: Château Haut Dina 2010  Castillon Côtes de Bordeaux

description: Made from Cabernet Franc, this opens with scents of purple flowers, toasted oak, exotic spice and red currants. The firmly structured palate doles out blackberries, black cherries, ground black pepper, clove, vanilla and mocha alongside velvety tannins. Drink through 2020.
title: Amantis 2010 Iperione Red (Toscana)

description: This is an aromatic wine, one of a series of four wines featuring the major red grapes of the Dão. It is ripe, fruity with soft tannins and juicy acidity. Red berry fruit and a balanced structure give a fragrant wine that is ready to drink.
title: Quinta de Lemos 2010 Alfrocheiro (Dão)

description: Here's a wine made with an unusual blend of Sangiovese, Syrah, Alicante and Carignan grapes. It opens with aromas of toasted oak, espresso and charred earth. The dense, extracted palate offers dried blackberry, baked plum, tobacco and licorice alongside a backbone of firm tannins. The fruit is drying up so drink soon.
title: La Mozza 2010 Aragone  (Maremma Toscana)

description: Crushed violet, iris, spiced plum and raisin aromas come together on this. The structured palate offers dried black cherry, blackberry jam, licorice and nutmeg alongside firm, fine-grained tannins. It closes on a note of roasted coffee bean.
title: Fidora 2010 Monte Tabor  (Amarone della Valpolicella)

description: Berry and cherry aromas are fruity and clean. This blend of Carignan, Grenache and Syrah is big on the palate but also tannic, acidic and fierce. Black plum, blackberry and tomato flavors finish crisp and firm.
title: Portal del Montsant 2010 Brunus Red (Montsant)

description: Fine and ripe, this is a sweet—while also complex—wine from Graham's flagship estate. Its tannins lead easily to ripe flavors of black plum and berry juice, resulting in a wine that is firm, full bodied, fruity and generous. It needs to age.
title: W. & J. Graham's 2010 Quinta dos Malvedos Vintage  (Port)

description: This is a big, strong Cabernet, flooding the mouth with intensely ripe blackberry, cassis liqueur, dark chocolate and white pepper flavors. It's tannic and acidic, and the oak hasn't been integrated at all with the fruit. Everything should begin to come together by 2017.
title: Knights Bridge 2010 Cabernet Sauvignon (Knights Valley)

description: This is a smooth, wood aged wine that comes from the Mouchão estate. It has rich red fruits, finely integrated tannins and a solid structure. It has an open, generous feel to it, given shape and aging potential by the dry core and fruity acidity. Drink from 2015.
title: Mouchão 2010 Dom Rafael Tinto Red (Alentejo)

description: An alluring nose of deep pear and peach lead this exciting variety from Axia. On the palate, the curvy, creamy waves of peach and citrus are followed by a clean finish. A tasty white that will pair well with fish, chicken salad.
title: Alpha Estate 2010 Axia Malagouzia (Florina)

description: This opaque, thick-looking wine opens with crusty, toasty aromas on top of an array of ripe berry notes. This is a blocky, saturated blend with flavors of herbs, tomato sauce and salt. A solid feeling finish with mild heat and burn is the lasting impression.
title: Melipal 2010 Malbec-Petit Verdot-Cabernet Franc Red (Mendoza)

description: Notes of light grass, green melon rind and a touch of green pepper unfold in the bouquet, while the palate boasts more citrus-based flavors of lime and tart gooseberry. This is bright and lively, with a short, mouthwatering finish.
title: Recanati 2010 Sauvignon Blanc (Shomron)

description: This bottling has a creamy texture, with pleasant, simple flavors of citrus and buttery oak.
title: Franciscan 2010 Cuvée Sauvage Chardonnay (Carneros)

description: Virtually identical with Pinot Noir, and quite a good one at that. Shows cool-climate influence in the translucent color, vital acidity and pure flavors of raspberries and persimmons, accented with smoky oak.
title: Novy 2010 Van der Kamp Vineyard Pinot Meunier (Sonoma Mountain)

description: This Syrah leads with standard berry and chocolate aromas. The mouthfeel is largely fresh, and while there's creamy oak as part of the flavor package, there's also good berry character and mint.
title: San Huberto 2010 Reserva Syrah (Argentina)

description: Rich and loaded with exceptional fruit power, this is dominated by the Champoux Vineyard's classic black-cherry character. Accents of clean earth, violet, tar and caramel show extraordinary depth, framed by firm and muscular tannins.
title: Sineann 2010 Block One Cabernet Sauvignon (Columbia Valley (OR))

description: Oak, toast and scorched soil aromas lead the nose along with a whiff of dry underbrush. The assertive, robust palate shows dried cherry, licorice and the heat of evident alcohol alongside bracing tannins that leave an astringent finish. While the tannins need time, this lacks the fruit richness to stand up to lengthy aging.
title: La Velona 2010 Riserva  (Brunello di Montalcino)

description: Leafy underbrush, sunbaked earth and a toasted note lead the way. The lean one-dimensional palate is a bit shy on freshness, offering dried sour cherry, raisin and a hint of dark spice alongside dusty, not very persistent tannins. Drink sooner rather than later to capture the remaining fruit.
title: Castellani 2010 Brunaio  (Brunello di Montalcino)

description: Certified-organic Cabernet Sauvignon makes up 100% of the wine. A study in red currant and clove, it's very much on the savory spectrum, with severe swathes of wood tannin and a bite to the finish.
title: Viluko 2010 Split Rock Cabernet Sauvignon (Sonoma County)

description: The white wine appellation of Pacherenc du Vic-Bilh covers the same area as the red Madiran. This dry wine, a blend of Petit Courbu and Petit Manseng is fat, full in the mouth with a smooth texture and rich acidity. It is ready to drink.
title: Alain Brumont 2010 Les Jardins de Bouscassé  (Pacherenc du Vic Bilh)

description: Similar in every respect to the '09 vintage, a softly ripe wine that's drinkable now despite Petite Sirah's firm tannins. It's very delicious, showing candied blackberries, cherries, currants and chocolate.
title: Vina Robles 2010 Estate Petite Sirah (Paso Robles)

description: A wine that has all the freshness of the Gascony region while bursting with some extra weight of fruit. There is a fine citrus and apricot concentration, just a hint of toast and enticing fresh acidity.
title: Producteurs Plaimont 2010 L'Empreinte de Saint-Mont Blanc  (Saint-Mont)

description: Made with 85% Merlot blended with varying amounts of Petit Verdot, Sangiovese and Cabernet Sauvignon, this opens with aromas of truffle, underbrush, baked plum and dark cooking spice. The palate delivers licorice, roasted coffee bean and blueberry extract alongside astringent tannins that dry out the palate. While the tannins are still aggressive, the fruit richness is starting to dry out.
title: Poggioventoso 2010 Fuochi Red (Toscana)

description: This very earthy wine with a barnyard character has lost its fruit. It does have structure but it is not likely to lose its animal edge.
title: L. Tramier & Fils 2010  Gevrey-Chambertin

description: Delightful rose and violet perfume mingles with ripe plums and cherries on this lavish Hudson Valley Baco Noir. On the palate, penetrating fruit is balanced with brisk acidity and a lean, elegant profile. Finishes with just a hint of green leaves.
designation: Old Vines Masson Place Vineyard Pulteney Farm
title: Hudson-Chatham 2010 Old Vines Masson Place Vineyard Pulteney Farm Baco Noir (New York)

description: Nothing like an Argentine Malbec, but not a bad wine. It's marked by high acidity that gives it a real bite, and exceptional dryness. Yet there's a good core of blackberry fruit. A rich steak will tease out the sweetness.
title: McManis 2010 Malbec (California)

description: Bright raspberry and leather aromas set up a tight palate ringing with acidity. That creates a narrow flavor profile with drawn cherry and currant flavors. A minty, lightly herbal finish carries notes of leather and oak-based bacon, which frankly seems a bit forced given the simplicity of the wine.
title: Calcu 2010 Cabernet Sauvignon (Colchagua Valley)

description: This red blend of 34% Babic, 33% Plavina and 33% Lasin has delightful aromas of tart cherry pie fresh out of the oven. There's lots of bright fruit and fresh acidity in the finish.
title: Bibich 2010 Riserva R6 Red (North Dalmatia)

description: Fragrant and refined, this opens with intense floral aromas and wafts of perfumed berry. The elegant, structured palate delivers layers of red cherry, juicy raspberry, cinnamon and a mineral note alongside firm, fine-grained tannins.
title: Franz Haas 2010 Pinot Nero (Alto Adige)

description: There's no oak at all on this savory young Sauvignon Blanc, which delivers its richness directly to the palate, undiluted. Those flavors are of green fruits: melons, apples, limes and the slightly sour, flowery tang of gooseberries. Fine acidity makes it all brisk and mouthwatering. An outstanding Sauvignon Blanc for today's ethnic fare, at a great price.
title: Husch 2010 Sauvignon Blanc (Mendocino)

description: Only their second vintage of Finger Lakes Riesling, Long Island's Macari Vineyards makes quite a splash with this beautifully honeyed yet dry and fruitful wine. Richly textured with notes of yellow freesia and preserved peach, it finishes elegantly on a subtly astringent black tea note.
title: Macari 2010 Riesling (Finger Lakes)

description: Black olive dominates on the nose in this blend of 75% Syrah, 12.5% Grenache and 12.5% Mourvèdre, all from Kick Ranch. Lively red fruit is amplified by soft, medium-bodied tannins and a well-composed finish, with just enough earth and spice to go around.
title: Carica 2010 Kick Ranch Siren Red (Sonoma County)

description: Very delicious and savory if a little hot in alcohol, this has flavors of raspberry and cherry jam, buttery pie crust and vanilla that are flecked with red pepper flakes. It's not an ager, but it is an elegant, flashy wine for pairing with tuna, lamb or steak.
title: Pali 2010 Cargasacchi Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)

description: Gentle white blossom, white currant and Golden Delicious apple unite on an intriguing, subtly aromatic nose. Earthy, stony elements lend depth and resonance. This is so integrated that the delicious, ripe apple fruit is completely embraced by earth and subtle yet distinct autolysis. This is rich in style—aided by dosage—rounded and very elegant, with a sufficient structure for food pairing.
title: Hattingley Valley Wines 2010 Blanc de Blancs Chardonnay (England)

description: Blended from Williamson, Hells Canyon and Bitner vineyards, this is a light, airy Syrah, with notes of cranberries and orange rind. There's intrigue, but the wine comes off as a bit muddled.
title: Koenig Vineyards 2010 Three Vineyard Cuvée Syrah

description: The wine is 100% Cabernet Sauvignon, and it brims with varietal notes of dryness, strong tannins and black currant and dark chocolate flavors. It's still shut down, almost undrinkable for the hardness. But it could age well. Begin investigating it in 2018.
title: Olabisi 2010 Black Pearl Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

description: The best way to understand this wine is to consider a fine Pinot Noir, with its light, silky texture and brisk acidity. Then make it very tannic, and add a smoked meat, bacon note to the cherries and raspberries. With a dry, distinguished finish, it strongly suggests beef dishes, preferably augmented with cheese.
title: Soquel Vineyards 2010 Luna Matta Vineyards Nebbiolo (Paso Robles)

description: This is a lovely wine, based on Cabernet Sauvignon, but comprised of all five classic Bordeaux varieties. It's smooth in tannins and soft in acidity, with luxurious black cherry, blueberry, cassis, plum and new oak flavors. Drink now–2018.
title: Dry Creek Vineyard 2010 The Mariner Meritage (Dry Creek Valley)

description: This is seriously spicy on the bouquet, where a mix of earth, pepper, herbal notes and cassis set up a stocky body with a condensed feel and ample oak. Heavy black-fruit flavors are hard at first but settle well, leaving mint and licorice notes along with a dark, toasty, herbal finish.
designation: Ecos de Rulo Single Vineyard El Chequén Estate
title: Viña Bisquertt 2010 Ecos de Rulo Single Vineyard El Chequén Estate Cabernet Sauvignon (Colchagua Valley)

description: Another solid effort from this young winery, the Cabernet is augmented with a 12% mix of Merlot, Petit Verdot and Cab Franc. Compact and clean, it puts on a display of well-structured cranberry, cherry and cassis fruit, lightly tasting of savory earth, pencil lead and bark.
title: Elevation Cellars 2010 Cabernet Sauvignon (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Made from Sangiovese, with 5% Merlot and 5% Canaiolo and Mammolo, this features aromas that recall forest floor, wet leaves and baking spices. The forward palate offers vanilla, plum cake and baking spices but the fruit richness is starting to dry up. Enjoy soon.
title: Lunadoro 2010  Rosso di Montepulciano

description: This full-bodied red opens with aromas of smoke, flint and dark spice. On the structured palate, clove and black pepper add depth to ripe morello cherry while firm close-grained tannins provide the framework. Drink 2018–2028.
title: Amarano 2010 Principe Lagonessa  (Taurasi)

description: Cherry and plum aromas are basic and candied, while the palate on this Prieto Picudo-based wine is perky and acidic. The flavors run dilute and herbal, with cherry pie and tart raspberry notes. Lingering acidity makes the finish both fresh and sharp.
region_1: Vino de la Tierra de Castilla y León
title: Bodega Melgarajo 2010 Valdeleña Tinto Roble Prieto Picudo (Vino de la Tierra de Castilla y León)

description: This opens with a heavy black-cherry aroma that's topped with a hint of leaf and a whiff of plastic. It feels medicinal and tastes like a mix of cherry cough syrup, oak and mint.
title: Ombu 2010 Pinot Noir

description: This is purple in color, with a saturated, rubbery aroma that folds in notes of leather, chocolate and blackberry. It's full and lush, with flavors of lemon, grainy oak, coffee and charred black fruit. Dark, oaky and charred on the finish, it needs a lot of air. Predominantly Malbec, with small additions of Syrah and Cabernet Sauvignon.
title: Koyle 2010 Reserva Malbec (Colchagua Valley)

description: Creamy, rich and spicy, this has potent pineapple- and orange-jam flavors, with lots of the sweetly buttered toast you get from oak barrel aging. It seems a little sweet in the finish, but it's elegant and refined.
title: Pech Merle 2010 Chardonnay (Russian River Valley)

description: Waxy lemon and apple skin aromas meld with spring blossoms on this bright, citrusy Pinot Blanc sparkling. While it's aggressively foamy, sunny tangerine acidity shines on the palate. Finishes crisply on a zesty lemon note.
title: Lieb 2010 Blanc de Blancs Brut Pinot Blanc (North Fork of Long Island)

description: With some weight, this has raspberry flavor to go with its sweetness. Berry fruits dominate in a wine that is full in the mouth.
title: Château de Fesles 2010  Rosé d'Anjou

description: Simple, broad and slightly oily at first, this has generous melon and apricot aromas. There is enough natural acidity to brighten the palate.
title: Paladin 2010 Pinot Grigio (Delle Venezie)

description: This is an estate-grown Chardonnay, with flavors of vanilla, tropical pineapple and guava that are surrounded by an oaky character and a lemony finish.
title: LangeTwins 2010 Reserve Chardonnay (Clarksburg)

description: A ripe, full wine that has almost too much weight, it offers a very rich texture and firm tannins. The blackberry jelly fruits are dense and powered by a fruity feel. Drink from 2016.
title: Château Larteau 2010  Bordeaux Supérieur

description: Aged for four years in old wood before bottling, this ripe, soft and sweet wine is typical of the Borges style of Port. It is fruity with ripe damsons, prunes and fine concentration. With acidity and touches of tannin to give the wine shape, it is balanced and ready to drink.
title: Borges 2010 Late Bottled Vintage  (Port)

description: Snappy and edgy as you give it a sniff, with piercing rhubarb, red cherry and peppery aromas. Shows a bit of cushion and softness in the mouth along with spicy, slightly herbal flavors of red plum and raspberry. Spicy on the finish, with citrusy acidity and dry oak notes.
title: William Cole 2010 Columbine Special Reserve Estate Bottled Pinot Noir (Casablanca Valley)

description: Sweet, floral and delicate, this fresh Moscato d'Asti opens with fragrant aromas of white flower and honey followed by stone fruit and honey. It offers full, generous mousse on the finish.
title: Enrico Serafino 2010  Moscato d'Asti

description: Leafy underbrush, wet soil, prune and spicy plum cake aromas lead the nose on this robust wine. The palate delivers ripe black plum, blueberry extract, black pepper and bitter dark chocolate alongside chewy tannins. It finishes with a graphite note.
title: Gamba 2010 Le Quare  (Amarone della Valpolicella Classico)

description: This deep straw-colored wine has a nose of mango, caramelized pineapple and canned apricots. A strong backbone of minerality runs through the core, bolstering flavors of peach and tropical fruit.
title: Sonberk 2010 Ryzlink Rýnský (Moravia)

description: This is a big wine, broad and fruity. Keeping the firm tannins and minerality well behind the red berry fruit flavors, it already shows drinkability. This second wine from Poeira is also a wine that could age impressively, full and structured. Drink now and until 2020.
title: Poeira 2010 Pó de Poeira Red (Douro)

description: This opens with a touch of cherry backed by notes of exotic spice, leather and moist earth. It has a plush, succulent mouthfeel and crisp acidity.
title: Mauro Veglio 2010  Barbera d'Alba

description: This is the estate bottling, lush and round, with a Chardonnay-like mouthfeel from barrel fermentation. Flavors of citrus and apricot fruit, expressions of a cool vintage, pull it into sharp focus. A smattering of toast and butter threads through the finish.
title: àMaurice 2010 Sparrow Viognier (Walla Walla Valley (WA))

description: Hints of beeswax and lemon rind introduce this deeply mineral dry Riesling. It's delicately framed, with tart tangerine flavors that bristle with acidity. A hint of honey lingers softly on the finish.
title: Baron Knyphausen 2010 Kiedricher Sandgrub Erste Lage Riesling (Rheingau)

description: Allegrini's base Valpolicella blend stands out in a crowd thanks to its richer, darker and extracted overall appearance. It shows medium length and density with fresh fruit flavors and a touch of savory barbecue spice on the close.
title: Allegrini 2010  Valpolicella

description: This is definitely high in alcohol, with a notable chili pepper burn, but that's the winery's style. Without that ripeness, you wouldn't have these intense, briary blackberry and mulberry flavors. Notes of chocolate, raisin, anise liqueur and exotic spice provide additional complexity. This is a Zin that needs rich, oily meats and cheeses to balance it out.
title: Frank Family 2010 Zinfandel (Napa Valley)

description: A confected wine that shows polished wood and mint flavors but little fruit. It puts on a great show, but without depth.
title: Château Brethous 2010 Prélude  (Cadillac Côtes de Bordeaux)

description: Mint aromas speak of the wood used in aging this wine. With some burnt tannin character, it's perhaps not a wine that will show great fruit.
title: Château Haut Canteloup 2010  Blaye Côtes de Bordeaux

description: Good price for a nice everyday Riesling. It's almost sweet enough to qualify as a dessert wine, but let's call it off dry, with orange, apricot and honeysuckle flavors, brightened with zippy acidity.
title: J. Lohr 2010 Bay Mist White Riesling (Monterey County)

description: Light in color and soft in the glass, this well-built wine is bursting in red berry and cherry, with aromas of violet and oak and a finish laced in cherry-vanilla. Structured and finessed, it's impressive for hearty pairings like pork or game,
title: Ferrari-Carano 2010 Sky High Ranch Pinot Noir (Mendocino Ridge)

description: That this wine is all Merlot is the reason both for its jammy richness and its high alcohol. It gives a wine that is out of balance, with pepper, spice and wood dominating the super-ripe black cherry fruits. It's unlikely to age for long.
title: Château de Brondeau 2010  Bordeaux Supérieur

description: Charbono is an old variety in Napa Valley, now rarely grown. This wine is bitterly tannic, yet the blackberry, cherry and mocha fruit is so strong, it breaks through the astringency, making the wine drinkable now. But it will age for at least a decade, maybe two. Try as an interesting alternative to Cabernet Sauvignon or Merlot.
title: OnThEdge 2010 Frediani Vineyard Charbono (Calistoga)

description: Ripe and tropical on the nose, this off-dry wine is chock-full of fleshy cantaloupe and nectarine flavors, balanced by nervy acidity and bitter tea-leaf notes. Full bodied and a tad blowsy, this finishes long on a floral note.
title: Keuka Spring 2010 Gewürztraminer (Finger Lakes)

description: Smoky flavors give the fruit a firm feel, with the black currant juiciness somewhere in the background. The wine is dark and tannic, dense and austere.
title: Château Lafleur-Gazin 2010  Pomerol

description: Dark and dense, this has loads of ripe fruit and leather aromas on the bouquet. It shows a soft, supple side on the palate, with a tangy touch of black licorice on the finish.
title: Tenuta Gorghi Tondi 2010 Coste a Preola Nero d'Avola (Sicilia)

description: This has spice and dried fruit notes, plus firmly structured tannins. The refreshing acidity partners with the fruit, creating a wine that still needs a few years to age.
title: Château Laulerie 2010 Le Raisin Merlot (Bergerac)

description: This is a dense, concentrated and oak-aged wine with delicious, juicy black fruits as well as elegant tannins and acidity. That makes for a wine that's balanced, developing well in its richness. Drink now and until 2020.
title: Château Famaey 2010 Fût de Chêne Malbec (Cahors)

description: Juicy blackberry jam, anise and oak flavors abound in this dry, tannic Cabernet. The palate is a little sharp in texture, and it is unlikely to age well, so drink now.
title: Donati 2010 Claret (Paicines)

description: From one of the few estate properties in this new appellation comes anise-flecked aromas of blackberry puff pastry, mixing up ripe fruit with buttery oak. This is a very approachable Syrah, with dark fruits and pepper spice that will appeal widely, showing why Ballard Canyon may be the variety's best hope for popular acceptance.
title: Harrison Clarke 2010 Cuvee Charlotte Syrah (Ballard Canyon)

description: Lots of toasty oak can't hide the fact that the underlying wine has a strong vegetal note, with some apricot and pineapple flavors. Tasted twice.
title: Thomas Scott 2010 Grand Reserve Chardonnay (Sonoma County)

description: The small Mazoyères-Chambertin Grand Cru creates structured wines with plenty of texture and tannins. This is brooding, firm and very concentrated, with its fruit set way behind the dark tannins and spice note. It will take at least 10 years to start to mature.
title: Domaine Perrot-Minot 2010  Mazoyeres-Chambertin

description: This shows something of Margaux's—the neighboring appellation—delicacy, allied with firm, dry tannins. It has the dense structure and ripeness characteristic of the vintage, with a rich black-fruit flavor and acidity. It's a wine for long-term aging.
title: Château Cambon la Pelouse 2010  Haut-Médoc

description: DFJ's venture into the Alentejo has produced a full-bodied wine packed with ripe plums and berry fruits. Tannins are rich, integrated into the fruits. A touch of earthiness needs to blow off before the wine is ready to drink in 2015.
title: DFJ Vinhos 2010 Monte Alentejano Red (Alentejano)

description: An awkward wine, with some sharp, green tea notes that stick in the mouth. Otherwise, you'll find firm tannins and jammy blackberry fruit.
title: Hearst Ranch 2010 Babicora Malbec (Paso Robles)

description: This wine seems to show some of the effects of the cool 2010 growing season, where ripeness and botrytis presented issues in some areas. Notes of tobacco, ash and orange peel surround a sweet cherry core accented by grainy tannins.
title: Sagelands 2010 Cabernet Sauvignon (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: With cedar and tobacco aromas, and vivid black cherry and black currant flavors, this full-bodied red has lots of fruit character and complexity to match its firm tannins and bold texture. A welcome spice component from oak aging starts in the aroma and continues through the finish.
title: Naggiar 2010 Estate Cabernet Franc (Sierra Foothills)

description: Ripe pineapple, orange and tropical fruit flavors are covered with oaky notes of buttered toast and butterscotch, resulting in this lavish if rather one-dimensional wine. It plays to the popular taste, at the expense of complexity.
title: Hestan 2010 Chardonnay (San Francisco Bay)

description: The nose on this Cabernet is scratchy and closed, with cherry, cassis and herbal aromas. The palate is forward and medium in body, with rough tannins and rubbery, oak-based flavors of blackberry and herbs. Overall this wine is drying but solid, with freshness but also a clampy feel.
title: Morandé 2010 Gran Reserva Cabernet Sauvignon (Maipo Valley)

description: Smooth and rich, its creamy texture is rounded out with a toast note. Flavors of peppered apricot and green plum work well together to give a warm, concentrated wine that shows all of its ripe fruit up front.
title: Château Routas 2010 Coquelicot Viognier-Chardonnay (Vin de Pays Var)

description: A solid, foursquare and tannic wine, it shows the structure of the vintage. It is dense and firm, the wood aging giving dryness. Hints of the juicy fruity side of the wine are there, so wait until 2015 for it fully to balance out.
title: Yvon Mau 2010 Grand Palais  (Bordeaux Supérieur)

description: This has soft tannins, ripe fruit and fresh acidity, with a dominant berry flavor. For a Nuits-St-Georges, this is quite open.
title: Laroze de Drouhin 2010 Les Charmottes  (Nuits-St.-Georges)

description: Kracher's sweet Scheurebe is consistently a remarkable wine. It's gorgeously opulent and tightly textured at the same time. Spice, sweetness and acidity combine harmoniously and seamlessly. Age this great wine and don't drink before 2017.
title: Kracher 2010 Zwischen den Seen Nummer 10 Trockenbeerenauslese Scheurebe (Burgenland)

description: Elegant mineral, citrus and spice aromas lead into complex layers of sea salt and lemon flavors. Overall this is balanced, but with a bracing edge that's refreshing and refined.
title: Argyros 2010 Estate Argyros Assyrtico (Santorini)

description: Smooth and softly textured, this is an attractive red wine. It has ripe berry fruits, gentle tannins that are enough to bring out the wood and spice flavors. Give the wine two years.
title: Roger et Didier Raimbault 2010  Sancerre

description: A pretty Viognier with oaky pear and apricot aromatics, lean, dry and crisp in style with plenty of depth on the finish that will appeal to fans of Condrieu. This wine will be available in 2013.
title: Terre Rouge 2010 Viognier (Fiddletown)

description: Already showing some maturity, this is a finely perfumed wine, its fruitiness tempered by secondary aromas and flavors that bring out herbs and an edge of steel. The aftertaste with its juicy acidity, is totally refreshing.
title: Hubert Meyer 2010 Riesling (Alsace)

description: Opaque purple and thick in the glass, this wine has inky, supercharged aromas of ripe blackberry and spice. It feels amped up but balanced, with more intensity and clarity than what most other $18 reds offer. The flavors of wild berry, mild herb and chocolate finish in a rich, toasty wrapping.
title: Bodega Norton 2010 Reserva Malbec (Mendoza)

description: The wine is sweet, very rich and smooth. Firm tannins are cushioned by the ripe berry and red plum fruits to give a spicy wine with touches of wood, finishing with pepper from the alcohol. Drink from 2017.
title: Château Haut Prieur 2010  Blaye Côtes de Bordeaux

description: Big and rich, this wine is exuberantly fruity with a sweet juicy core of ripe blackberry. Big, bold and forward with well-integrated tannins, this is likely to age quickly; try in 2–3 years.
title: Companhia das Quintas 2010 Herdade da Farizoa Reserva Red (Alentejano)

description: There's an extreme tightness to this top-end bottling from a premiere vineyard in this newish appellation. The nose is very lean with dark spice, blueberry and purple fruit, and the palate is extremely tannic, making any other flavors fall away. Only time will tell whether the wine is a success.
title: Star Lane Vineyard 2010 Astral Red (Happy Canyon of Santa Barbara)

description: With a gold color and heavy, pithy aromas that are becoming oxidized, this is a mature, highly stylized Albariño. It's also a touch flat, with flavors of bitter citrus and nectarine pit. Overall this is more leesy and mature than vital and outwardly delicious.
title: Zarate 2010 Balado Albariño (Rías Baixas)

description: Dark berry, mineral and baked notes control the nose on this blend of Cabernet Sauvignon, Carmenère, Petit Verdot and Syrah. This is full, round and firmly tannic, with oaky, minty flavors of black fruits and licorice root. Mocha, tobacco, chocolate and coffee flavors are nice on the finish. Drink through 2018.
title: Casas del Toqui 2010 Codigo del Toqui Red (Cachapoal Valley)

description: A note of barely ripe wild berry is quickly overtaken by a strong, green-herb core. It turns bitter and slightly medicinal on the finish.
title: Airfield Estates 2010 Syrah (Yakima Valley)

description: Ripe cherry and blackberry flavors mark this Merlot. It's dry and rich in tannins, with a rugged mouthfeel and an overripe, raisiny taste.
title: Trentadue 2010 Block 500 Merlot (Alexander Valley)

description: Inky dark-fruit aromas are blanketed by oaky scents that settle on caramel and vanilla. This four-grape blend of Bordeaux varieties is both creamy and acidic, with herbal notes along with spice, wild berry and leafy flavors. Snappy, crisp acidity hangs around on the finish, creating a racy sensation. Drink through 2018.
title: Lagarde 2010 Henry Gran Guarda N°1 Red (Mendoza)

description: This smells rubbery and a bit gamy, with meaty berry aromas. Tannic, with gaps in the mouthfeel, it has flavors of prune and herb, which are not excessively sweet or raisiny. The finish is dry, tannic and steady.
title: Pradorey 2010 Roble  (Ribera del Duero)

description: There's a vegetal smell and taste to this Pinot Blanc that detracts from the otherwise fine orange and peach flavors. After a long string of superior bottlings of this variety since 2003, Kynsi simply ran into the most challengingly cold vintage in years.
title: Kynsi 2010 Bien Nacido Vineyard Pinot Blanc (Santa Maria Valley)

description: A healthy mix of boysenberries and strawberry preserves form the core aromas, with touches of spiced toast and purple flower. Delicate tannins lend the palate an open feel, and a green streak cuts through the center. The flavors would benefit from a boost of concentration.
title: Gadino 2010 Antiche Viti Reserva Cabernet Franc (Virginia)

description: Spicy generic berry aromas come with an herbal component and a note of licorice root. This feels tight and compact, but not overly ripe. Berry fruit flavors are herbal and the slightest bit green, with a black-pepper note in front of a rubbery finish with clamping tannins.
title: Finca La Emperatriz 2010 Crianza  (Rioja)

description: Aged 18 months in French oak, this wine is moderately aromatic, with notes of blueberry and mushroom. It's syrupy in feel yet has dry tannins.
title: Koenig Vineyards 2010 Williamson Vineyard Sangiovese

description: Merlot leads the pack in this blend of six Bordeaux varieties with a dash of Syrah (5%). The fruit is taken from five different vineyards across Washington and Idaho. It has notes of candied cherry, licorice and ash, with tart flavors and dry, astringent tannins.
title: Clearwater Canyon 2010 Renaissance Red (America)

description: Some creamy yeasty tones are supported by background notes of banana and apricot and give this Soave Classico a rich, generous mouthfeel. Pair this wine with pasta, eggplant sauce and mozzarella.
title: Inama 2010 Vin Soave  (Soave Classico)

description: A Bordeaux-style blend made primarily from Merlot, plus Cabernet Sauvignon, Cabernet Franc and Malbec, this is layered with waves of blackberries, blueberries, currants, dark chocolate and oak flavors. The tannins are pure and refined. Production is 2,000 cases.
title: Cameron Hughes 2010 Lot 335 Meritage (Oakville)

description: Initially, this is tight, minerally and structured, but as the wine opens, intense dark fruits and rich tannins surface, creating an opulent, juicy character. The vineyard is planted with 75% Merlot and 25% Cabernet Franc, and it's the combination in 2010 that gives such a vital, rich and structured selection.
title: Château Ambe Tour Pourret 2010  Saint-Émilion

description: Inky violet-red in color, this wine has aromas of raspberry, black cherry, vanilla and mixed baking spices. Flavors of black cherry, raspberry, coffee and toffee are equally bright on entry and at the finish, with a nice mellow period in the middle. The silky finish is marked by a vivid salted toffee splash.
title: Or Haganuz 2010 Namura Single Vineyard Special Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon (Galilee)

description: The addition of 28% Grenache brings a punchy, upfront fruitiness to the umami-soaked Syrah. Tangy orange/raspberry fruit is concentrated and fills the mouth; then the peppery, earthy Syrah backbone brings flesh to the finish. Beautifully proportioned.
title: àMaurice 2010 Boushey Vineyard Syrah-Grenache (Yakima Valley)

description: An absolutely first-class Chardonnay, although with production at only 100 cases, it may be somewhat difficult to find. It shows near-perfect balance of acidity, oak, minerality and lees, with a touch of sour-lemon candy drop.
title: Crosby Roamann 2010 Chardonnay (Carneros)

description: A finely structured, still young wine with an edge of austerity. It does have a rich core of red fruit, giving a slightly juicy character, but with its tannins it will always be a firm wine, dense and serious.
title: Herdade de São Miguel 2010 Colheita Seleccionada Red (Alentejano)

description: Here's a fragrant and fresh expression of Grillo that shows sharp, green tones of Granny Smith apple, kiwi, cut grass and lemon soda. The wine is clean and zesty, and will make your mouth pucker.
title: Feudo Disisa 2010 Grillo (Sicilia)

description: They muscled up this Sangiovese with some Cabernet, which gives it a fuller body and sturdier tannins than usual. Nonetheless, it has Sangiovese's silkiness and red cherry and leather flavors.
title: Frank Family 2010 Reserve Sangiovese (Rutherford)

description: This is a broad, full-bodied Carmenère with leathery, foresty aromas as well as berry and cassis. The palate feels wide, grabby and hefty, while the flavors run toward meaty and toasty, with dark berry, herb and mildly bitter notes. On the finish, it overflows with heat, chocolate and peppery flavors.
title: Koyle 2010 Royale Carmenère (Colchagua Valley)

description: A blend of Nero d'Avola and Nerello Cappuccio, this delivers a tangy, spicy feel, plus polished aromas of blackberry, almond and cured meat. Pair this easygoing selection with pepperoni pizza.
title: Viticultori Associati Canicatti 2010 Calìo Red (Sicilia)

description: The first impression is of strawberry preserves, not jammy but with the texture and loose-knit fruit flavor of a fruit spread. Behind it are lip-licking veins of licorice and cola, finishing with dusty tannins. Chehalem dust? Don't rule it out.
title: J. Christopher 2010 Lia's Vineyard Unfiltered Pinot Noir (Chehalem Mountains)

description: A bright, tart, spicy wine with plenty of grip and detail, this threads flavors of herb, stem and earth into the substantial blackberry and cassis fruit. It's nicely woven together, penetrating and punchy.
title: Dusted Valley 2010 Cabernet Sauvignon (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Minty, scratchy aromas of cherry and berry air out well. This feels chewy yet fresh in the mouth, with plum and cherry flavors matched by good overall intensity and balance. A controlled finish tastes dusty and loamy; drink through 2017.
title: Viña Herminia 2010 Crianza  (Rioja)

description: This shows the varietal's spicy, earthy aromas in a fresh way. The mouthfeel is crisp, while herbal, peppery flavors blend properly with the spice and plum notes typical of Carmenère. The finish is brambly and drifts toward spunky red fruit.
title: De Martino 2010 Legado Reserva Carmenère (Maipo Valley)

description: This Cabernet Sauvignon-dominant blend boasts leather, tobacco leaf and black pepper flavors, giving it muscle and a rustic edge, the tannins and fruit mellowed with age.
title: Ehret 2010 Bella's Blend Cabernet Sauvignon-Cabernet Franc (Knights Valley)

description: Impenetrable ruby in hue, this heavy-hitting blend of 51% Petit Verdot, 20% Merlot, 15% Cabernet Franc and 13% Cabernet Sauvignon offers tight aromas of cassis, char and espresso, with hints of blackberry and tobacco. The palate demonstrates impressive balance between its flesh, acids and tannins, while boasting notable concentration. A note of black raspberry extends onto the finish.
title: The Williamsburg Winery 2010 Lord Botetourt Limited Red (Virginia)

description: High alcohol and exceptionally ripe fruit are the hallmarks of this mountain vineyard. In this Zinfandel, they express themselves as blackberries, cherries and mocha that have a roasted edge, and a Port-like heat that is by no means unpleasant. But you do want to drink this Zin with dishes rich in beef, cheese and olive oil.
title: Charter Oak 2010 Monte Rosso Vineyard Zinfandel (Sonoma Valley)

description: This late release is dry verging on astringent, with a tight shell of herb and stem. Inside a good core of tart fruit—cranberry and raspberry—still seems young and suggests that decanting and/or further bottle age will see this wine continue to improve.
title: Domaine Loubejac 2010 Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: Thick and syrupy, with candied raspberry and cherry flavors. A simple, soft little Pinot Noir that tastes as sweet as a pastry.
title: Jackhammer 2010 Pinot Noir (Central Coast)

description: This smells of latex and cinnamon. On the palate it's light and lean, with dilute flavors of cherry pie and unusual Middle Eastern spice. Grating and acidic on the finish.
title: Jordi Miró 2010 Maceració Carbónica Garnatxa Negra Garnacha (Terra Alta)

description: As its name indicates, “barriques” (barrels), this is that rare Pinot Blanc that is aged in wood. There's a mere hint of toast, the main thrust of this delicious wine being its pear and honeyed apple flavors, with acidity cutting through at the end.
title: Domaine Ostertag 2010 Barriques Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

description: Mushy black fruit characteristics lead on the nose and mouth, and don't really subside until the slightly astringent finish takes hold. Medium weight with a firm structure; drink now.
title: La Forge Estate 2010 Unfiltered Cabernet Sauvignon (Pays d'Oc)

description: Very juicy aromas of blueberry pie with a buttered crust and vanilla ice cream bring great promise to this Malbec. It carries through with a very round and broad mouthfeel and rich plum flavors with cracked black pepper. The tannins are a tad astringent.
title: Opaque 2010 Malbec (Paso Robles)

description: Black cherry, blueberry and blackberry combine for a rewardingly rich, ripe nose, layered with flecks of graphite and chai-like spice. The palate is driven at first by black soil elements, evolving into cooked cherries, tar and bitter root vegetables, yet possessing a strawberry lightness as well. This is extremely complex and as fresh as any more recent vintages.
title: Ken Brown 2010 Rancho La Viña Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)

description: Full bodied and plushly textured, this is an opulent wine that's approachable now, but should last through 2025. It's tight and reticent when poured, then opens in the glass to reveal raspberry fruit, with subtle baking spice and cacao.
title: Pierre Henri Morel 2010 Lieu-Dit Pignan Grenache (Châteauneuf-du-Pape)

description: Made entirely from Cabernet Sauvignon, this wine opens with aromas of red berries, sweet baking spice, grilled herbs and a whiff of cedar. The structured but almost weightless palate doles out ripe red currants, red raspberries, licorice, mocha and white pepper. Supple, smooth tannins weave alongside the juicy flavors. Drink through 2016–2022.
title: Mazzei 2010 Philip Cabernet Sauvignon (Toscana)

description: This is a rich blend of Aragonez and Touriga Nacional that fills out broad black fruit and acidity with soft tannins and a ripe texture. Rich and juicy, it's ready to drink.
title: Roquevale 2010 Tinto da Talha Grade Escolha Red (Alentejano)

description: This is a ripe, rounded and firmly tannic wine. It has weight and a dense texture, with a black plum flavor. With its fruits, sweet tannins and solid structure, it's likely to age well over several years.
title: Château Larroque 2010  Bordeaux

description: Smoky, ripe fruit and dark spice notes are enhanced by bright tones of blackberry and blueberry. Pair this with potato gnocchi in meat sauce or orecchiette with smoked ricotta cheese.
title: Villa Mottura 2010 Le Pitre Primitivo (Salento)

description: Oaky and ripe up front, this offers graphite, inky berry and cedary aromas. A plump, full-tannin palate has flavors of black cherry, plum and cassis along with integrated spice notes. It's woody, tannic and demanding on the finish.
title: Familia Cassone 2010 Obra Prima Reserva Cabernet Sauvignon (Luján de Cuyo)

description: This smooth, supple Bordeaux-style blend is principally Merlot, yielding a lush mouthful of blue and purple fruits. Light chocolate and soft leather bring added richness through a mouthcoating finish.
title: Dusty Cellars 2010 DCW Spartan Red (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: This earthy wine features aromas of truffle, ripe plum, leather and just a whiff of barnyard. The palate offers black cherry, crushed raspberry and notes of baking spice alongside assertive tannins.
title: Demarie 2010  Barbaresco

description: Perfumed in leathery cinnamon-cola and cedar with gobs of tannin, it will get you through a party or midweek meals.
title: LangeTwins 2010 Estate Grown Cabernet Sauvignon (Lodi)

description: This pleasing effort is light but interesting. It first introduces flavors of strawberry and rhubarb, with a dash of pepper. A whiff of root vegetables does not interfere with the overall enjoyment, and a lick of chocolate lifts the finish.
title: Lenné Estate 2010 Jill's 115 Pinot Noir

description: This marks a real departure for Michael David, a producer that tends to make its wines big and bold. This soft, hardly oaked, lightly tannic Cinsault is from a very special old vineyard. With a smooth texture and a core of red cherry and cranberry flavors, it is light-bodied and very floral.
title: Michael David 2010 Bechthold Vineyard Ancient Vine Cinsault (Lodi)

description: This wine has a fine definition of minerality that's combined with tense structure. There is a hint of wood, a flavor of fresh-peeled apple and a taut, steely edge. Layers of acids mark the finish.
title: Jaffelin 2010  Puligny-Montrachet

description: Grenache Blanc is a newcomer to the Northwest, and what little exists is rarely offered in a varietal wine. Here it's spicy and phenolic, with sharp definition. Green apple and diverse yellow fruits are in the mix, with a hint of mineral and plenty of bracing acidity.
title: McCrea Cellars 2010 Boushey Vineyard Grenache Blanc (Yakima Valley)

description: Not surprisingly, this young wine has dense, potent tannins, along with plenty of stem and herb accents. It's balanced texturally, and has rather acidic, young fruit flavors of cranberry and raspberry. At the moment it is the most chewy and tannic of all the 2010 reds from Animale.
title: Animale 2010 Petite Sirah (Yakima Valley)

description: Rich and chocolaty, with blueberry and blackberry jam flavor, this is high in alcohol, but it is delicious nonetheless.
title: Sextant 2010 Wheelhouse Zinfandel (Paso Robles)

description: A little thin in flavor, this is nonetheless nice and dry, with gentle tannins framing cherry, herbal tea, spice and sandalwood flavors. Drink up.
title: Rutherford Ranch 2010 Merlot (Napa Valley)

description: Made with Sangiovese and 5% Canaiolo, it opens with aromas of wet soil, meat juices, spice and black fruit. The palate holds black cherry and white pepper but it has a short finish and lacks complexity. On the other hand, it's easy-drinking and thoroughly enjoyable now.
title: Castello di Querceto 2010 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: This relatively low-alcohol Zinfandel offers dusty blue fruit and piles of earthiness around a core of juicy texture. Straightforward on the finish, with a hit of herb and fresh farm.
title: Witching Stick 2010 Fashauer Vineyard Zinfandel (Anderson Valley)

description: This sweet dessert wine is riding the Moscato wave. It's clean and brisk in acidity, with flavors of oranges, honey and vanilla. Nice picnic wine, or drink with a weekend brunch.
title: Delicato 2010 Domino Moscato (California)

description: Quite hard and almost impenetrable at first, this slowly opens to give a glimpse of compact, dried blueberry fruit and a dash of dark chocolate. The tannins are hard and herbal also. Aeration will help.
title: Oak Knoll 2010 Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: Nutty and toasty on the nose, this blend of Mourvèdre, Syrah and Petit Syrah is medium to full bodied, and it offers a dark raspberry flavor, plus firm tannins and supporting acids.
title: Page Springs 2010 El Serrano Red (Arizona)

description: Based on Cabernet Sauvignon, this Bordeaux-style blend will make you take a second look at Paso Robles. It's softly tannic and enormously complex, offering waves of black cherries, currants, licorice, mocha and sweet oak. Really beautiful to drink now, and should develop bottle complexities over the next six years.
title: Daou 2010 Soul of a Lion Cabernet Sauvignon (Paso Robles)

description: Easy and gentle in mouthfeel, this has soft, velvety tannins, but the flavors are big and complex. It offers waves of blackberry and blueberry jam, anisette liqueur, milk chocolate, bacon and white pepper as well as a smoky, creamy touch that melts into a long, spicy finish. It's high in alcohol, but wears it well.
title: Hendry 2010 Block 28 Zinfandel (Napa Valley)

description: Leather, game, underbrush, dried herb and ripe black-skinned fruit aromas take shape in the glass. The big, chewy palate has a rustic charm, offering fleshy black cherry, game, truffle and a hint of cake spice wrapped in tightly packed tannins.
title: Verbena 2010 Riserva  (Brunello di Montalcino)

description: A broad, rather soft example of rosé, this blend of Grenache, Syrah and Carignan should be consumed over the next few months.
title: Louis Bernard 2010 Rosé (Costières de Nîmes)

description: Shows its California origins in very ripe, lush and soft flavors of raspberries and cherries, creamy chocolate frosting and red currants. Plenty of oak adds wood spice and toast. A big, generous wine, flashy and incredibly rich, if lacking a bit in subtlety. Drink soon.
title: Donum 2010 Estate Grown Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

description: Aromas of forest floor, leather, espresso, spice and grilled herbs lead the nose. The palate offers toasted oak, roasted coffee bean, dried sage, raw red cherry and licorice alongside astringent tannins that need time to integrate fully. Drink after 2018.
title: Poderi Luigi Einaudi 2010 Terlo  (Barolo)

description: This lively, crisp and tantalizing wine emphasizes fresh green apple aromas, vivid citrus flavors of lemon and grapefruit, and a rather full body from energetic bubbles. An attractively tart mouthfeel turns creamier with each sip. It does just what a brut style is supposed to in terms of refreshment and lip-smacking flavors.
title: Schramsberg 2010 Brut  (Anderson Valley)

description: With sweet flavors of honey, pineapple, mango and pear, followed by an oak-driven buttered toast note, this delivers more for the price than many Americans will expect. Zippy acidity keeps everything clean and balanced.
title: Elements by Artesa 2010 Chardonnay (Napa County-Sonoma County)

description: Aged in stainless steel for a fresh, zesty feel, this tangy Ribolla Gialla offers aromas of peach, mature melon and Golden Delicious apple. The wine is smooth and creamy and would make a perfect match to spicy appetizers.
title: La Viarte 2010 Ribolla Gialla (Colli Orientali del Friuli)

description: Vegetal notes of asparagus mingle with accents of chalk and sweet grass on the nose, while ruby grapefruit and underripe mango flavors burst in the mouth. Vibrant and clean; drink now.
title: Plaisir De Merle 2010 Sauvignon Blanc (Western Cape)

description: Charred earth, toast, grilled porcini, cured meat and mocha aromas lead the nose. The juicy palate offers mouthfuls of ripe black cherry, raspberry jam, licorice and menthol framed in big, velvety tannins. Drink 2018–2025.
title: Collelceto 2010 Elia Riserva  (Brunello di Montalcino)

description: Classic Sangiovese aromas of wild berry, just-turned soil, leather and a hint of underbrush unfold in the glass. The bright, juicy palate offers layers of rich wild cherry, black raspberry, cinnamon and pipe tobacco framed in firm, velvety tannins. A licorice note closes the finish. Drink 2020–2030.
title: Crocedimezzo 2010 Riserva  (Brunello di Montalcino)

description: This is one of the darker, fuller-bodied, richer rosés on the market. It almost has the weight of a red wine. Dry and zesty in acidity, it features powerful flavors of cherries, strawberries, cola and spices. A great wine for beef, pork and tuna. Don't drink it too cold.
title: Fort Ross 2010 Fort Ross Vineyard Rosé of Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)

description: Tannins, acidity, black fruit, everything is in its right place in this wine. It has the structure of the vintage, with a series of dry, smoky bacon flavors, the weight apparent.
title: Georges Duboeuf 2010 Belles Grives  (Morgon)

description: Ripe, luscious blackberry and bramble bush notes are seductive on the nose of this rather big, powerful Cabernet Franc. The palate is forward as well, with rich black-fruit flavors accented by sweet spice and vanilla, along with a fresh swathe of green on the finish.
title: Lakewood 2010 Cabernet Franc (Finger Lakes)

description: This fragrant Gewürztraminer from one of the best producers in northern Italy offers aromas of jasmine and honeysuckle, with background tones of apricot and candied exotic fruit. It's creamy and rich on the finish.
title: Colterenzio 2010 Atisis Cornell Gewürztraminer (Alto Adige)

description: Intense on the nose, this has spice, leather and ripe berry aromas. It feels clean, plummy, juicy and bright, with lifted red and black fruit flavors. Showing chewy tannins and additional juiciness on the finish, it's very nice and delivers the goods.
title: Pascual Toso 2010 Reserve Barrancas Vineyards Malbec (Mendoza)

description: This has somewhat acrid aromas of animal and farmyard, while the palate offers a very ripe plum flavor that's just short of being overripe, as well as vanilla and chocolate sensations. It finishes on a bitter note.
title: Rignana 2010  Chianti Classico

description: Tight, tannic and undrinkable now because it's so young, this 100% Cabernet needs a great deal of time to come around. Right now, it's hard as nails, a wine whose astringency stings the gums despite impressively intensely ripe blackberry jam and black currant flavors. It should be great in 2020–2025.
title: Corley 2010 State Lane Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon (Yountville)

description: This estate-grown, single-vineyard, 100% varietal bottling is smooth, rich and forward. Scents of baking spices, caramel and toast introduce a full and lengthy palate loaded with black cherry and cassis fruit. A steely firmness to the wine anticipates further development in the bottle.
title: Abeja 2010 Heather Hill Cabernet Sauvignon (Walla Walla Valley (WA))

description: Leathery in tobacco and black currant, this wine leaves a bold imprint of toasty tannins.
title: Brutocao 2010 Bliss Vineyard Merlot (Mendocino)

description: This savory selection offers a spicy bouquet of black pepper, nutmeg and clove, along with blackberry and cherry notes. It has a refined and vibrant palate, with black cherry, pepper and spice notes framed by silky tannins and refreshing acidity.
title: Novaia 2010 Vigneto I Cantoni  (Valpolicella Classico Superiore)

description: A blend of Tempranillo, the seldom seen (in Washington) Graciano and Grenache, this wine is direct and fruity with notes of raspberry, char and barrel spices. It's light in style with elegant fruit flavors and grainy tannins with the oak approaching too intrusive.
designation: H/H Estates Michael Andrews Coyote Canyon Vineyard
title: Coyote Canyon 2010 H/H Estates Michael Andrews Coyote Canyon Vineyard Red (Horse Heaven Hills)

description: Leather, menthol, ripe dark berry, pressed blue flower and a balsamic note emerge on this robust red. The full-bodied palate delivers mature wild cherry, pomegranate, star anise and clove set against a tannic backbone. You'll also detect the warmth of alcohol on the close. Drink 2018–2023.
title: Fornacina 2010 Riserva  (Brunello di Montalcino)

description: Subdued dried herb, vanilla and dill aromas mix with notes of baking spice. The red-fruit flavors are light and elegant, winnowing toward the finish.
title: Bunnell 2010 Clifton Hill Vineyard Syrah (Wahluke Slope)

description: This Lodi Cabernet is priced to sell, but it has a sweltering swath of green pepper.
title: Peirano 2010 Heritage Collection Cabernet Sauvignon (Lodi)

description: This wine is mostly Syrah, which gives juicy blackberry, plum and chocolate-covered raisin flavors. The slight percent of Viognier brings acidity and an uplifted floral note to this easy-drinking wine.
title: Coquelicot 2010 Sixer Syrah (Santa Ynez Valley)

description: Borra's blend of 80% Petite Sirah, 10% Syrah and 10% Mourvèdre has three strong personalities vying for attention with Petite Sirah definitely dominant in juicy, blueberry fruit and a core of dusty tannins, with just a suggestion of meaty game. Structured this is a nice wine to pair with a rich risotto or leg of lamb.
title: Borra 2010 47.5 Degree Red (Lodi)

description: This conveys aromas of mature dark fruit and leafy underbrush. The firm palate displays stewed plum, ground pepper and a hint of dried sage alongside extremely astringent tannins that cut short the finish. Tried twice with the same notes.
title: Donatella Cinelli Colombini 2010  Brunello di Montalcino

description: So broadly delicious now, it hardly seems worthwhile to age this wine, but don't let it fool you. The blackberries, currants, dark chocolate, minerals and oak will only transform into something seamlessly complex with 8–10 years in the cellar. The winery's Reserve bottling has established itself as one of the most consistently fine, ageable Cabs in Napa Valley.
title: Whitehall Lane 2010 Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

description: The wine comes from a small, seven-acre property. This 2010 is structured with a mineral character and chewy tannins. It is well balanced; the blackberry fruit synchronizes well with the dry texture and fresh acidity. Age for 4–5 years.
title: Château Graves de Pez 2010  Saint-Estèphe

description: This pure Merlot is made from fruit that was sourced from Bacchus, Conner Lee, Dionysus and Klipsun vineyards. Sappy, spicy and loaded with black fruits, this delivers plenty of flavor across the rich midpalate. Notes of black cherries, coffee grounds, licorice and smoke all come into play.
title: Arbor Crest 2010 Four Vineyards Merlot (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: This is a smooth and polished wine, with herb and new wood aromas. It has juicy red-berry flavors, and the tannins push forward. Structured, with intense acidity, this is ready to age for at least 5–6 years.
title: Domaine Jessiaume 2010 Gravières Premier Cru  (Santenay)

description: Delicious and wonderful to drink now. Too bad more Syrahs aren't this good. The wine is dry and smoothly tannic, with deep, long flavors of blackberries, currants, smoked meat, pepper and sandalwood and a medium-bodied fullness.
title: Rusack 2010 Ballard Canyon Estate Syrah (Santa Barbara County)

description: Light in color and soft in the glass, this well-built wine is bursting in red berry and cherry, with aromas of violet and oak and a finish laced in cherry-vanilla. Structured and finessed, it's impressive for hearty pairings like pork or game,
title: Ferrari-Carano 2010 Sky High Ranch Pinot Noir (Mendocino Ridge)

description: The nose isn't very expressive but the firm palate delivers red berry, menthol and baking spice. Assertive refined tannins provide the backbone. Give this time to fully develop.
title: Palladino 2010 S. Bernardo Riserva  (Barolo)

description: Made from 100% Cab, this wine is boldly fruity with flavors of black cherries and currants, along with toasted, caramelized marshmallows. The tannins are refined and there's good acidity for balance.
title: Mi Sueño 2010 Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

description: With the typical intense acidity of 2010, this is a taut, fruit- and mineral-driven Chablis. Its fruitiness is balanced by judicious toast and vanilla flavors. Age for 2–3 years.
title: Damien et Romain Bouchard 2010 Vaillon Premier Cru  (Chablis)

description: Made from grapes sourced from Domaines Devillard's 12 acres of premier cru Mercurey vineyards, this rich, weighty wine has ripe red fruits that are layered with new wood and tannins. A structured effort that's meant to age for five years and more.
designation: Château de Chamirey Les Cinq Premier Cru
title: Domaines Devillard 2010 Château de Chamirey Les Cinq Premier Cru  (Mercurey)

description: This inky and opaque Chianti Classico is made in an international style, offering intense oak and vanilla aromas. The palate shows overripe plum, espresso and green bell pepper flavors. While it starts off plush, it has short length with a drying, tannic finish.
title: Podere La Cappella 2010  Chianti Classico

description: This Merlot isn't very complicated, but it does have the soft juiciness you want in the variety. The flavors of cola and red cherries finish dry and spicy.
title: Roth 2010 Merlot (Alexander Valley)

description: An eccentric wine, very high in alcohol, yet there's a strange vegetal element. Otherwise, it's all about sweet blackberry jam, mushu plum sauce, white pepper and charred bacon. The green veggie note, which winemaker Matthias Pippig describes as “savage”, persists into the finish.
title: Sanguis 2010 1/1 Syrah (Santa Barbara County)

description: While dainty in structure, this light-footed Riesling is intense and concentrated, with ripe stone-fruit and tangerine flavors. Nervy lemon-lime--like acidity and shades of slate and steel add depth and balance.
title: Keuka Spring 2010 Riesling (Finger Lakes)

description: A complex blend of Chardonnay, Pinot Noir and Weissburgunder, this is a rich, spicy wine, dry in style and with some tannins. It has weight and density, but doesn't lose its creamy, fresh texture and acidity. It should age another year in bottle to get the best out of this serious wine.
title: Steininger 2010 Burgunder Sekt Sparkling (Österreichischer Sekt)

description: A great value wine from Sicily present on restaurant lists across southern Italy, La Segreta is a famously food-friendly red blend that offers light berry, cherry, almond and spice aromas. The easy mouthfeel pairs with pasta, risotto, veal or roast chicken.
title: Planeta 2010 La Segreta Red (Sicilia)

description: Juicy, jammy, tasting of red licorice and bright blackberry fruit, with dusty undertones and a bite of coffee on the finish, this is a good-value Zinfandel from old Lodi vines.
title: Campus Oaks 2010 Old Vine Zinfandel (Lodi)

description: This wine calls itself “a bit slutty” in its marketing materials and yes, like a high-priced call girl, it's long on perfume and smoke. From there, this gigantic, mind-stopping wine is mouth-filling in vanilla, blackberry, oak and chocolate. You'll need a cigarette after you're done.
title: Michael David 2010 Lust Zinfandel (Lodi)

description: One word describes this Chardonnay: fabulous. It's really everything a California Chardonnay can deliver. Ripe tropical fruit, pear, green apple and honey flavors are wrapped into buttery, smoky oak. The richness is brightened by crisp acidity and a touch of minerals, showing its pedigree with every nuance. Drink soon, to enjoy its youthful beauty.
title: Rochioli 2010 Sweetwater Vineyard Chardonnay (Russian River Valley)

description: Dry and silky, this is a good Pinot Noir, though it's a little too ripe, which gives the blackberry and cherry notes a pruny, hot taste. Try it with beef stew and prunes to echo the flavors.
title: Roadhouse Winery 2010 Orange Label Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

description: Soft and fruity, this has ripe black-cherry and blackberry compote flavors, plus plenty of oak influence. Drink now.
title: Rutherford Ranch 2010 Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

description: Beautifully crisp and fresh, this is a young, tightly textured wine that is already so refreshing. Its red fruit character is just a part of the mineral and tannin texture and the pure line of fragrant acidity. Very pale in color, this sophisticated wine could do with some aging. Drink from 2018.
title: Louis Roederer 2010 Rosé  (Champagne)

description: Floral red-fruit aromas get heavier with airing. Feels thick and full, with soft tannins and mild acidity. Tastes oaky but not overoaked, with caramel, vanilla and standard red-berry and plum flavors. A touch stewy on the finish, with a resiny oak flavor hanging in there.
title: Quintay 2010 Q Pinot Noir (Casablanca Valley)

description: If you haven't already tried Pecorino, Colle dei Venti is a good place to start. This boasts loads of peach, exotic fruit, pineapple and pear, with a medium body, good structure and tartness.
title: Caldora Vini 2010 Colle dei Venti Pecorino (Terre di Chieti)

description: There's a touch of spice in this rounded, ripe wine. It's dry, with flavors of baked apple and cinnamon. Full, rich and flavorsome.
title: Domaine Ernest Burn 2010 Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

description: Accompanying the raspberry and cranberry are green mint and vegetal notes. Made from a single vineyard, it shows a nice silkiness and dryness, with crisp acidity, but that lack of generosity lowers its score.
title: Nuggucciet 2010 Pinot Noir (Santa Maria Valley)

description: Finely structured and aromatic, this is initially dense before opening up with ripe yellow fruit, becoming balanced by orange- and tangerine-like acidity. It has a young, zesty texture that pairs with the rich concentration, promising to age over 3–4 years.
title: La Chablisienne 2010 Monte de Milieu Premier Cru  (Chablis)

description: There's a fresh, almost spicy quality to the nose, with fresh fruit, acacia flower, honey and crushed white pepper. The wine is steady and smooth on the palate, with a creamy texture and a foamy mousse.
title: Isolabella della Croce 2010 Valdiserre  (Moscato d'Asti)

description: A soft and simple Cabernet, with jammy blackberry, currant and sweet oak flavors. The finish is long and spicy. Drink up.
title: Summerland 2010 Cabernet Sauvignon (Paso Robles)

description: This Cabernet Franc-dominant blend, with additional splashes of Cabernet Sauvignon, Petit Verdot and Malbec, leads with earthy tones of menthol, black-currant leaf and tobacco. There's ample black-fruit flavors to support the herbal core—think blackberry, plum and cassis—all framed by medium tannins and a clean, peppery finish. Drink now–2016.
title: Raats Family 2010 Red Jasper Red (Coastal Region)

description: Light aromas of red cherry and raspberry can be discovered upon deep nosing, but the fresh garrigue, tree bark and raw cocoa nib notes steal the show. A hint of vanilla-strawberry sweetens up the close. Drink now.
title: La Forge Estate 2010 Unfiltered Pinot Noir (Pays d'Oc)

description: Touches of unripe vegetal aromas and flavors compromise this wine, despite some jammy blackberry and cherry flavors. Because of its thinness, its high alcohol content makes it feel hot. Drink now.
title: Turley 2010 Ueberroth Vineyard Zinfandel (Paso Robles)

description: This is one of the best Bordeaux blends now available from Paso Robles. Based on Cabernet Sauvignon, it's enormously ripe in blackberry jam, currant, chocolate and cedar flavors that finish lush and spicy. The tannins are strong, but negotiable with barbecue.
title: Écluse 2010 Ensemble Lock Vineyard Red (Paso Robles)

description: Right away you know this Syrah-Petit Verdot is a powerful wine. The bouquet is pointed and blasts away with cherry, berry, spice and oak aromas. It's thick, pushy and full-bodied, with minty, toasty oak flavors running side by side with blackberry. On the finish, it's more charred and toasty, with peppery spice and some heat. Drink now–2015.
region_1: Vino de la Tierra de Castilla
title: Finca Constancia 2010 Syrah-Petit Verdot (Vino de la Tierra de Castilla)

description: Comprised of 73% Grenache, 14% Mourvedre and 13% Syrah, Terre Rouge's L'Autre blend is a smooth rendition of lavender, tobacco and savory red plum—a touch of violet lurking within there too. Well intregrated and savory, with tightly woven tannins, this is a great example of how these grape three varieties can work together.
title: Terre Rouge 2010 L'Autre G-S-M (Sierra Foothills)

description: An extra year in bottle may cut down on the crispness of a Muscadet, but it does bring out so many attractive flavors. This wine boasts nuttiness and floral acidity that complements notes of white fruits and red apples. Screwcap.
title: Château de la Ragotiere 2010 Vieilles Vignes Sur Lie  (Muscadet Sèvre et Maine)

description: High alcohol is the price you pay for the exceptional ripeness of this rich, lush Pinot Noir. It's so forward in cherry, red currant, cola and mocha flavors, but finishes totally dry. Great acidity brings a zesty balance to this deliciously drinkable Pinot.
title: De Loach 2010 Thornton Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)

description: Since this is 100% Sauvignon Blanc, it is intensely crisp, with grapefruit flavors and bright acidity that lead into a lemongrass aftertaste. It's clean and fresh, and is likely to mature quickly.
title: Château Couhins-Lurton 2010  Pessac-Léognan

description: This basic, oaky red wine tastes overripe and raisinated with notes of bitterness.
title: Heibel Ranch 2010 Lappa's Red (Napa Valley)

description: A blend of Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Syrah and Petit Verdot, this is generous in terms of its aromatic intensity and persistence. The wine opens with tones of espresso, dark berry, sweet spice and chocolate. The mouthfeel is smooth, rich and opulent.
title: Castello di Bolgheri 2010 Varvàra  (Bolgheri)

description: Intense, generous aromas of white flower, passion fruit, citrus blossom and crushed stone comprise the nose. This blend of Sauvignon Blanc and Chardonnay offers impressive intensity and a long, spicy finish.
title: Le Macchiole 2010 Paleo White (Toscana)

description: Currently dominated by tannins, this wine is rich and structured, and it demonstrates the potential of its huge fruit and weight. The final note of this age-worthy wine is smokiness from wood aging.
title: Domaine Champy 2010  Corton-Rognet

description: This wine shows the structured side of Gevrey-Chambertin. The vineyard is situated at the opening of a small side valley, a location that allows the grapes to ripen slowly. This is elegant and structured, and it's full of solid tannins as well as cherry and red berry flavors. Age this for 7–8 years.
title: Albert Bichot 2010 Lavaux Saint-Jacques Premier Cru  (Gevrey-Chambertin)

description: Crisp and elegant in feel, this has apricot and peach flavors that are balanced by the tight structure and the green-fruit-like acidity. The layers of toast have integrated with the ebullient fruit character and the taut, minerally finish. For aging 3–4 years.
title: Olivier Leflaive 2010 Dents du Chien Premier Cru  (Saint-Aubin)

description: Thin in fruit and acidic, but with vigorous oak, this Chard will appeal to fans of buttered toast, popcorn and vanilla. Underneath that are pineapple and orange flavors.
title: Merriam 2010 Chardonnay (Alexander Valley)

description: With bright red fruits and acidity, this shows structure, piles of red cherry flavors, followed by firm tannins.
title: Mommessin 2010  Beaujolais-Villages

description: Showing up-front fruit, with tense acidity, this is all about freshness. There's a cool, textured feel to the wine, more plum skin than juice.
title: Paul Beaudet 2010 Domaine Romy  (Beaujolais)

description: A soft wine, with diluted berry and cherry flavors.
title: Cannonball 2010 Cabernet Sauvignon (California)

description: Structured, this offers earthy aromas of ripe berry, game, leather and anise. On the palate, notes of licorice, white pepper, clove and grilled herb add depth to the black cherry core. Firm tannins and nervy acidity provide an age-worthy framework. Drink 2020–2035.
title: Cantina del Pino 2010 Albesani  (Barbaresco)

description: Showing the long hang time flavors of the cool 2010 vintage, this shows fabulous depth of red berry fruit, with all kinds of licorice, wet stone, spice and sandalwood complexities. It boasts a silky texture, with brisk Central Coast acidity and even a dusting of tannins.
title: Kori 2010 KW Ranch Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)

description: This wine is chunky and firm, and its lively red fruit brings freshness. With its dry core and touch of toast on the finish, this could age for a couple of years.
title: Jaffelin 2010  Nuits-St.-Georges

description: A glass of pretty aromatics, grapefruit and orange blossom mostly, with a range of depth and texture that speaks of serious wine. Made from organic grapes.
title: Yorkville Cellars 2010 Randle Hill Vineyard Sauvignon Blanc (Yorkville Highlands)

description: Very herbal Sauvignon Blanc, crisp and tangy, with flavors of lime and green apple skins. Fresh as well as structured, with a final zest of acidity.
title: Château les Arromans 2010  Bordeaux Blanc

description: This is on the dry side of medium dry, very soft, with flavors of nuts, lemon and pear juice. The finish is bright with acidity.
title: Pierre Chainier 2010 Clos de Nouys Demi-Sec  (Vouvray)

description: You can't beat this price for a Pinot Grigio with natural freshness and pristine aromas of stone fruit and citrus. The nice thing is that it tastes Italian made, and not like many of the commercial, generic Grigios on the market today.
title: Kirkland Signature 2010 Pinot Grigio (Friuli Grave)

description: A pale salmon color, this rosé shows ripe notes of cocoa-dusted cherry and strawberry alongside sweet citrus oil. Creamy and full in the mouth, it's a richer rosé that can stand up to heartier fare.
title: Morgenhof 2010 Fantail Pinotage Rosé (Stellenbosch)

description: This wine has varied over the years, but with the cool 2010 vintage, it really shows how fine its terroir can shine. A full degree of alcohol lower than the 2009, it's bone dry, brisk in acidity, and stimulating in its minerally taste and texture—like a lick of cold steel. There is a fortunate streak of Meyer lemon candy that lends enough richness to satisfy. Reminiscent of a fine Chablis, it's drinking well now.
title: Knights Bridge 2010 West Block Chardonnay (Knights Valley)

description: While the wine is clean and refreshing, its texture lacks definition. It has a soft, bright finish.
title: Domaine Bernard Moreau et Fils 2010  Bourgogne

description: This is a yeasty wine that shows a bitter almond character. It has an unattractive hard edge.
title: Domaine Faiveley 2010  Chablis

description: Sourced from the Sundance Vineyard, this is a soft and oaky Chardonnay, with a round, buttery finish. Best enjoyed in the next year or two.
title: Page Cellars 2010 Autumn Chase Chardonnay (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Made entirely with Montepulciano, this opens with earthy aromas of tilled soil along with notes of black fruit and espresso. The linear palate delivers bright red cherry, notes of mocha and somewhat astringent tannins. Straightforward and simple, this should be enjoyed soon.
title: Conte Leopardi 2010 Fructus  (Rosso Conero)

description: This intense wine opens with an alluring fragrance of crushed flower, vineyard dust, bright berry, leather, cooking spices and balsamic notes. The structured palate delivers ripe black cherry, prune, cracked black pepper, toast and licorice alongside vibrant acidity. The fruit richness stands up to the assertive tannins but it ends on a mouth-drying finish. Drink 2020–2040.
title: Marchesi di Barolo 2010 Sarmassa  (Barolo)

description: For the moment, the tannins are still firm and the generous red fruits have not yet penetrated the surface. The acidity is present, like a line of brightness that cuts through this richly tannic wine. This is a potentially opulent wine.
title: Olivier Leflaive 2010 Santenots Premier Cru  (Volnay)

description: This wine offers notes of woodspice, butterscotch, coffee and blackberry. The fruit flavors are lighter in style with mouthwatering acids and firm, grippy tannins that need some time or perhaps a big, juicy steak to tame them.
title: Henry Earl 2010 Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon (Red Mountain)

description: A very fine Sauvignon Blanc that's pure, fresh and crisp. This shows uplifted citrus fruit, gooseberry, date and spice flavors. It's an excellent example of the variety; only 387 cases were produced.
title: Stonestreet 2010 Sauvignon Blanc (Alexander Valley)

description: Lenaia Rosso (50% Merlot with Sangiovese and Colorino, vinified in cement) opens with foxy notes of crushed grape, root beer and bitter almond. It's short, crisp and fine on the finish.
title: Casa di Terra 2010 Lenaia Rosso Red (Toscana)

description: Notes of orange and lemon preserves on the nose lead into flavors of orange, lemon and vanilla on the palate. It finishes with a touch of spice.
title: Gentilini 2010 Cellar Selection Robola (Cephalonia)

description: A blend based on Cabernet Sauvignon and Petit Verdot, this is heavy, soft and fruity, delivering a blackberry flavor and a dry finish.
title: Tower 15 2010 The Swell Red (Paso Robles)

description: In 2007, this was produced as a late-harvest wine. In 2010, by contrast, it is a crisp, apple- and pear-flavored wine. It has bright fruit, lively acidity and just an edge of sweetness.
title: Emile Beyer 2010 Lieu dit Hohrain Pinot Gris (Alsace)

description: This is a rich and structured wine from Heifara Swatvagher. With wood aging giving the wine its toasty, spicy character, it is ripe and perfumed with black plum flavors and fresh with black currant acidity. The wine is only just beginning to be ready to drink and will certainly be better from 2018.
title: Château Saint-Sernin 2010 Prestige Malbec-Merlot (Cahors)

description: With an earthiness that weighs down the cherry, cola and currant flavors, this Pinot tastes a bit heavy. It's properly dry, with good acidity and modest alcohol.
title: Longoria 2010 Bien Nacido Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Maria Valley)

description: This is a herbaceous wine with dominant green fruit, followed by acidity and fine flavors of sweet currant, kiwi and thyme. It is fresh and ready to drink.
title: Domaine Fouassier 2010 Sur le Fort  (Sancerre)

description: The blend is Cabernet Sauvignon, Syrah, Merlot and Malbec. Cab Sauv brings rich black currants, as well as thick tannins. Merlot adds softer notes of cherries and violets. The Malbec increases the tannins, along with notes of grilled meat bone that Syrah, with its peppery, beef-jerky meatiness, enhances. The result is a young, vigorous wine, dry and balanced in acidity. It may or may not age, but there's no reason not to decant it now and enjoy with grilled beefsteak.
title: Laird 2010 Jillian's Blend Red (Napa Valley)

description: The lightest of the dessert wines made by Claude Loustalot, this is fruity, fragrant and medium sweet, with refreshing acidity. The crisp texture combines hints of honey and apricot jam. Drink now.
title: Domaine Bru-Baché 2010  Jurançon

description: Smells different than most, with blueberry, carob and exotic sandalwood. Takes a dive on the palate, where it's sweet, syrupy and flat, almost like prune juice. Along those lines, it tastes pruny, with chocolate on the finish. Struggles to achieve balance.
title: Manos Negras 2010 Malbec (Mendoza)

description: Baked plum, molasses, balsamic vinegar and cheesy oak aromas feed into a palate that's braced by a bolt of acidity. A compact set of saucy red-berry and plum flavors features tobacco and peppery accents, while the finish is mildly green in flavor, with respectable weight and balance.
title: Felix Lavaque 2010 Felix Malbec (Cafayate)

description: This 100% Sangiovese opens with aromas that recall coffee, scorched earth and toasted oak. The fleeting palate offers espresso, mocha and a green note of powdered sage alongside astringent tannins. The fruit has mostly dried out.
title: Castello di Querceto 2010 La Corte Sangiovese (Colli della Toscana Centrale)
